channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Five coastal towns for a perfect winter escape in South Africa
item.description: South Africa offers a refreshing winter escape with its coastline, where quieter shores, dramatic seascapes, and whale watching replace the usual summer crowds. This piece highlights five coastal town...

item.title: Zanzibar to roll out standardised taxi meter and digital payment system to improve transport transparency
item.description: Zanzibar is planning to introduce a modern taxi metering and digital payment system aimed at improving transparency, service quality, and efficiency within the islands’ transport sector. The initiativ...

item.title: Turaki-led PDP relocates convention venue and endorses Goodluck Jonathan for 2027 election after police blockade
item.description: The Turaki-led faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) in Nigeria has reportedly changed the venue of its convention in Abuja after police officers blocked access roads leading to the original l...

item.title: Debate over Bill C-9 raises concerns about free expression and hate speech laws in Canada
item.description: The article by Conrad Black examines the controversy surrounding Bill C-9, also known as the Combating Hate Act, which has passed the House of Commons and is now under scrutiny for its broad definitio...

item.title: Concerns Over Nike’s Recycled World Cup Uniforms and the Limits of Chemical Recycling
item.description: Nike has introduced a new initiative ahead of the World Cup in which uniforms for 16 national teams are made using recycled materials derived from textile waste. The company claims the jerseys were pr...

item.title: Federal Judge Temporarily Blocks Trump Administration’s Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, established as part of a settlement involving former President Trump, his family, and the Tru...

item.title: Karachi hospitals report increase in stomach illness cases during Eid ul Adha due to heavy meat consumption
item.description: During the Eid ul Adha holidays, public hospitals in Karachi reported a noticeable increase in patients suffering from stomach-related problems. According to medical staff, emergency departments remai...

item.title: AUKUS partners launch joint underwater drone programme to protect seabed infrastructure
item.description: The United States, the United Kingdom and Australia have announced a joint initiative to develop advanced underwater drone technology under their AUKUS defence partnership. The uncrewed undersea vehic...

item.title: Forensic archaeology reveals burial sites and legacy of the Namibian genocide
item.description: This article explores how forensic archaeology is shedding new light on the Namibian genocide that took place between 1904 and 1908 under German colonial rule, during which tens of thousands of Ovaher...

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal Prepare for Champions League Final in Budapest
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal are set to meet in the 2026 UEFA Champions League final at Budapest's Puskás Aréna. PSG, the defending champions, aim to become only the second team after Real Madrid t...

item.title: Rep. Tom Kean Absent from Congress for Over 75 Days Amid Medical Issues, Sparking GOP Concern in Competitive New Jersey District
item.description: Republican Representative Tom Kean from New Jersey has been absent from Congress for more than 75 days due to a personal medical issue, according to reports. This prolonged absence has raised signific...

item.title: Ricky Stuart maintains stance on NRL refereeing concerns after Raiders loss
item.description: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart has reaffirmed his concerns about the consistency of NRL officiating following his side’s 30-22 defeat to the Dolphins, stating that a detailed review of the match ...

item.title: Ugandan Health Ministry Advises Ebola Survivors on Post-Recovery Precautions
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Uganda has issued guidance for men who have recovered from Ebola, advising them to abstain from sexual activity for up to six months or use condoms consistently. Health exper...

item.title: DCI Arrests TikToker for Offensive Posts on Utumishi Girls' Dormitory Fire
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has arrested David Onyango Elgon, popularly known as MC Adek Tatu, following public outrage over his social media posts perceived as insensitive after ...

item.title: Trump-linked festival struggles to replace performers after multiple withdrawals
item.description: A multi-day patriotic festival known as the “Great American State Fair,” organized by a group linked to former President Donald Trump under the Freedom 250 initiative, is reportedly facing significant...

item.title: Bulls secure URC semifinal spot with emphatic win over Munster
item.description: The Bulls put on a commanding performance at Loftus, overwhelming Munster 45-14 in the URC quarterfinals to book their place in the semifinals against Glasgow Warriors. From the outset, the Bulls’ bac...

item.title: Arsenal consider move for Khvicha Kvaratskhelia amid transfer speculation
item.description: Arsenal Football Club is being linked with a possible move for Napoli’s star winger Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, as reports suggest the London side is looking to strengthen its attacking options for the ong...

item.title: Sculpture of Lord Buddha by Sandhya Patnaik Unveiled at VIMS, Visakhapatnam
item.description: On Saturday, Visakha Institute of Medical Sciences (VIMS) in Visakhapatnam witnessed the unveiling of a remarkable sculpture of Lord Buddha, masterfully designed by the well-known artist Sandhya Patna...

item.title: KP Chief Minister requests GB Supreme Court to oversee free and fair elections
item.description: PESHAWAR: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi has formally written to the Chief Justice of Gilgit-Baltistan Supreme Appellate Court, Sardar Muhammad Shamim Khan, seeking judicial oversight...

item.title: Retired Nigerian Military General and Wife Abducted in Katsina
item.description: Dem una report say Major General Rabe Abubakar wey don retire as Director of Defence Information, and him wife don get kidnapped for Katsina State, for Matazu Local Government Area. Dem say armed men ...
